seoTitle: How to Edit a Wix Website After Publishing
metaDescription: Learn how to safely update a live Wix website's content and design without causing downtime for visitors.
intro: Publishing a Wix site isn't the end of the process — you'll likely need to make regular updates, and Wix lets you do this without taking your site offline. This guide covers opening your live editing workspace, making content and design changes, previewing them, and pushing updates live with the Publish button.

steps.heading: Open your live editing workspace
steps.body: From your Wix dashboard, open your site's core editor to begin making changes.

steps.heading: Understand drafts vs. live pages
steps.body: Know that changes you make in the editor stay as drafts until you publish, so your live site isn't affected until you're ready.

steps.heading: Edit your content
steps.body: Update paragraph text, swap button links, and replace outdated graphics as needed.

steps.heading: Adjust structural sections
steps.body: Update global site settings and layout grid blocks behind the scenes if needed.

steps.heading: Check mobile responsiveness
steps.body: Toggle the mobile view to confirm your changes look correct on smaller screens.

steps.heading: Preview and publish
steps.body: Run a full interaction check in preview mode, then click Publish to push your changes live instantly.

faq.question: Will my changes go live immediately while I'm editing?
faq.answer: No, changes stay as drafts in the editor and only go live once you click the Publish button.

faq.question: Does editing my site cause any downtime?
faq.answer: No, your live site keeps running normally while you edit, and updates only apply the moment you publish.

faq.question: Can I preview changes before publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, the built-in preview tool lets you check both desktop and mobile views before pushing anything live.

faq.question: How do I confirm my published changes actually took effect?
faq.answer: Open your live custom domain in a separate browser tab after publishing to verify the update appears correctly.
